date: # One row per customer conversation. The four marker columns are 0 or 1 (behaviour you OBSERVED
# not opinions you heard). desperation_score = their sum (0-4): 3-4 = desperate
name: 1-2 = needy

date: # past_behavior_quote = something they DID or said about the past (signal)
name: never "I would" (noise).

date: # FILLED EXAMPLE: rows 1-5 are the tech-forward single-site slice we later DROPPED (polite/needy
# no live pain). Rows 6-15 are the mid-market multi-site leafy-green who we narrowed toward.
# Row 7 and 14 carry the workaround signal: someone built a spreadsheet hack and even paid for it.

name: A. Reyes
past_behavior_quote: We wrote a Python script two years ago that spits out the day's cut list from seeding dates; it just runs.
notes: Already automated the job. Software-friendly but no live pain. Part of the slice we dropped.

name: M. Okafor
past_behavior_quote: I demoed a farm ERP last year then didn't buy; we're 9 people, I just tell them in the morning.
notes: 9 staff, single site. No coordination cost. Polite. Helped trigger the pivot.

name: J. Vance
past_behavior_quote: Our MES already sequences the racks; harvest falls out of that, nobody owns a separate schedule.
notes: Automated away inside an existing system. Calm. No pain to sell into.

name: S. Iyer
past_behavior_quote: I googled harvest scheduling software once, found nothing for our size, kept using a Google Doc.
notes: Searched, found a gap, didn't pay. 11 staff, single site. Gap real but pain mild.

name: D. Brooks
past_behavior_quote: We just walk the racks together at 7am; it's never been a thing we've had to fix.
notes: No search, no workaround, no charge. Confirms this slice doesn't bleed. Pivot away.

name: L. Tran
past_behavior_quote: Every morning at 5 I redraw the whiteboard and text three crew leads who cuts what; if I oversleep packout slips and we miss the retail cutoff.
notes: THE workaround signal: 5am whiteboard + texts. 2 sites, 34 staff. Desperate.

name: P. Castellano
past_behavior_quote: I built a 9-tab spreadsheet that figures cut order from seeding dates and crew size; I paid an Upwork freelancer 400 dollars to clean it up last spring.
notes: Workaround built AND money spent on it. 3 sites, 51 staff, Priva. Strong WTP precursor.

name: R. Mwangi
past_behavior_quote: When the lead is off I inherit her spreadsheet and dread it; I got the basil cut order wrong twice and we composted a whole rack.
notes: Workaround fragile and role-dependent (single-seat risk). 2 sites, 28 staff. Real cost: composted rack.

name: K. Nielsen
past_behavior_quote: I keep a laminated master sheet and rewrite it daily; I asked our climate vendor if Argus could do crew assignments and they said no.
notes: Active search went to the climate vendor and hit a wall. 2 sites, 40 staff. Desperate.

name: T. Alvarez
past_behavior_quote: We trialed a generic workforce-scheduling app for two months last year, paid for it, then cancelled because it knew shifts but not crops.
notes: Spent money on an adjacent tool, churned because it missed the crop layer. Pinpoints the wedge. 4 sites, 66 staff.

name: G. Santos
past_behavior_quote: My phone has 5am alarms labelled cut list site A and site B; I haven't taken a Monday off in over a year without training a backup for a week first.
notes: Desperation high and personal. 3 sites, 47 staff, Priva. Top beachhead candidate.

name: H. Park
past_behavior_quote: We have a shared sheet but half the crew leads ignore it and ask me in person, so I end up telling them anyway.
notes: Workaround exists but adoption weak; pain partly absorbed by manager. 2 sites, 31 staff. Needy.

name: C. Dubois
past_behavior_quote: We're opening a second site in spring; right now one site, the grower handles it, I expect it'll get messy.
notes: Pain anticipated, not yet lived (still single-site today). Polite. Re-interview after second site opens.

name: N. Haddad
past_behavior_quote: I run everything off a spreadsheet I email myself nightly so I have it on my phone at 5am; I once paid a college kid to build a little form for it.
notes: Workaround + small spend + nightly ritual. 3 sites, 38 staff, Argus. Strong.

name: B. Fischer
past_behavior_quote: After a week where two crews double-cut the same beds I made a colour-coded board and started photographing it each morning to keep a record.
notes: Triggered a workaround after a concrete failure (double-cut beds). 2 sites, 29 staff. Solid signal.
